{{nihongo|'''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'''|特選神名牒}} is a commentary on {{ill|Engi Shikishinmeichō|de|Engi Shikishinmeichō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shikishinmeichō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. It is a 32-volume work compiled by the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}}. Based on official histories, ancient texts, and the “Shrine Investigation Records” as well as “Shrine Details Registers” produced in each province during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era, the work, following the order of the Shinmeichō, records the enshrined deities, divine Ranks, shrine status, locations, and other particulars of each {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The editing appears to have been undertaken by {{ill|Kurita Kan|de|Kurita Kan|ja|栗田寛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita Kan|1=Kurita Hiroshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536170}}, {{ill|Konakamura Kiyomune|de|Konakamura Kiyomune|ja|小中村清矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakamura Kiyomune|1=Konakamura Kiyonori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536303}}, {{ill|Kosugi Sanemura|de|Kosugi Sanemura|ja|小杉榲邨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Sanemura|1=Kosugi Sugimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536257}}, {{ill|Inoue Yorimitsu|de|Inoue Yorimitsu|ja|井上頼圀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorimitsu|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535302}}, among others; however, due to the loss of related documents (see below), details remain unclear.<ref>In Kurita’s former library a draft of this work was preserved, and all manuscript copies dating from before the fire were in Kosugi’s own hand, with numerous later additions by Konakamura and Kurita. In addition, participation by then Ministry of Religion members such as {{ill|Tanaka Yoritomo|de|Tanaka Yoritomo|ja|田中頼庸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Yoritomo|1=Tanaka Yoritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162647}}; {{ill|Ochiai Naotoshi|de|Ochiai Naotoshi|ja|落合直澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochiai Naotoshi|1=Ochiai Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98734451}}; {{ill|Kubo Tokiji|de|Kubo Tokiji|ja|久保季茲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubo Tokiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; {{ill|Monoshū Takami|de|Monoshū Takami|ja|物集高見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshū Takami|1=Mozume Takami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537728}}; {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|de|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}}; {{ill|Nakamura Akika|de|Nakamura Akika|ja|中村秋香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Akika|1=Nakamura Akika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111081324}}; {{ill|Saruwatari Yōsei|de|Saruwatari Yōsei|ja|猿渡容盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruwatari Yōsei|1=Sawatari Hiromori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38279444}}; and {{ill|Hori Hidenari|de|Hori Hidenari|ja|堀秀成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Hidenari|1=Hidenari Hori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120309}} has been surmised.</ref> After its provisional completion in the 9th year of Meiji ({{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876}}), the handwritten manuscript was stored in the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs}} {{ill|Bureau of Shrines|en|Bureau of Shrines|WD=Q11590701|lt=Shrine Bureau}}, but in the 14th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} ({{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925}}) it was published as a western-style single-volume edition by {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
==Compilation History== ===Preliminary History=== In the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} era ({{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}}), as part of the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}, a directive was issued to all shrines nationwide requiring them to submit their genealogies to the local lords in charge. Subsequently, on June 10 of the second year, orders were given to the domains and prefectures to record the histories and shrine legends of the greatly revered major shrines; however, due to slow progress, on February 29 of the third year of Meiji a provisional measure was taken ordering that the portions relating to state‐funded shrines in each domain be submitted to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities}} by September of that year. These records were then compiled into the “Report on Revered Shrines.”
Furthermore, on October 28 of that leap year, due to the need to formulate shrine regulations, a more detailed survey of the shrines within each jurisdiction was ordered to the domains and prefectures (which eventually took shape as the “Records of Large and Small Shrines”), and based on that survey, in May of the fourth year the Ranks of shrines below the state‐funded level were defined (for details, see ‘’{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}’’).
===Compilation=== [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 175.jpg|thumb|200px|Chapter “Tōmi Country” from the western-style single-volume edition of “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 276.</ref>]] Because the shrine regulations of Meiji 4 established a hierarchical Ranking based on contemporary conditions, some shrines – such as certain {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} – were not granted any Rank (i.e. unranked shrines). Fearing their eventual abolition, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} (the successor to the Jingikan) initiated the compilation of this work.
On April 10 of the 7th year of Meiji, a petition was submitted to the senior officials of the {{ill|Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|de|Daijō-kan (Meiji-Ära)|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|lt=Daijō-kan|lt_ja=太政官|lt_de=Daijō-kan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} requesting the compilation of a work focusing primarily on the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. The proposal stated that the shrines to be included would be ceremonially enshrined during the two seasonal festivals (spring and autumn) in the three halls of the Imperial Palace – rendered as {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Shrine}} (at that time, they were enshrined at {{ill|Kensho|de|Kensho|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensho|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}) – and that their shrine grounds would be preserved in accordance with the standards applied to state‐funded shrines. On April 17, a draft imperial edict from the Daijō-kan, ordering each prefecture to conduct a survey of the shrines within their jurisdiction, was submitted. The Daijō-kan, fearing an undue administrative burden on the prefectures, consulted the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs}} on May 5. As expected, objections were raised on May 14 on the grounds of complicating local administration. Consequently, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} was instructed that while the compilation of this work was permitted, decisions regarding the ceremonial enshrinement at the shrine halls and the preservation of the shrine grounds should await its completion, and that the survey orders to the prefectures should be issued not by the Daijō-kan but by the Ministry of Religion (directive dated May 22).
In June, the Ministry of Religion issued orders to the prefectures to conduct a comprehensive survey of both the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, and to submit their reports by the end of September of that year. Incidentally, the survey covered the following 11 items: # Location of the main seat # Shrine name # Enshrined deity # Genealogy (including the shrine family line – {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family}}) # Date of enshrinement – {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement}} # Annual festival date # Building area of the shrine hall # Area of the shrine precincts (including the former precincts) # Former shrine domain – {{ill|Shrine Domain|de|Schreinbereich|ja|社領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} # Number of registered parishioner households # Distance from the governing authority
Within the Ministry, a compilation committee was established—centered on the Department of Shrines and Temples and the Department of Historical Research—but as the survey demanded even more detailed information than the shrine surveys conducted in Meiji years 2–4, reports were slow to be received even past the deadline, and despite repeated reminders, some prefectures repeatedly requested extensions. Although progress remained slow by August of Meiji 9, in January of the following year the Ministry of Religion was abolished, and despite some omissions, this work was provisionally completed in December of that year.<ref>It is said that Mie Prefecture ultimately did not complete its survey.</ref>
==Publication History== After its completion in Meiji 9, because the manuscript stored in the Ministry of Home Affairs was inconvenient to consult, plans were set in motion to publish it publicly in Taishō 11. While {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was preparing for publication, in September of Taishō 12 the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|de|Großes Kantō-Erdbeben|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} struck during printing. In the ensuing disaster, only four volumes of the original manuscript (the Ministry of Home Affairs version) survived, while all other related documents – including the aforementioned “Report on Revered Shrines” and “Records of Large and Small Shrines” – were completely destroyed.<ref>The only volumes that survived the disaster were Vol. 21 (Echigo/Sado), Vol. 22 (Tango/Tanba), Vol. 23 (Tajima/Inaba/Hakki), and Vol. 24 (Upper Izumo).</ref> Fortunately, the head of Isobe Kōyōdō evacuated with the 580-page paper type that had been approved for printing, and the manuscripts in process were recovered intact from the {{ill|Baggage|en|Baggage|WD=Q865054|lt=Baggage}}. Based on these, under the supervision of the Ministry of Home Affairs’ temporary appointee {{ill|Ota Akira|de|Ota Akira|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Akira|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, the text was proofread. Although collating it with the original was impossible, the “Shrine Details Register” was used as a reference to append the contemporary (Taishō-era) location and Rank of the shrines, and the work was published. According to the preface, although the original manuscript had included entries for the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}, these do not appear in the current edition.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tokusen Jimmyōchō]][[ja:特選神名牒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tokusen Jimmyōchō]][[ja:特選神名牒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tokusen Jimmyōchō]][[ja:特選神名牒]]
{{Multiple Issues|Source Documentation=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 08:03 (UTC)|Unreferenced=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 08:03 (UTC)}} [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho honbun cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 37.jpg|thumb|300px|‘Tokusen Shinmyōchō’ – Text of the western‐style single-volume edition<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 1.</ref>]]
{{nihongo|'''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'''|特選神名牒}} is a commentary on {{ill|Engi Shikishinmeichō|de|Engi Shikishinmeichō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shikishinmeichō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. It is a 32-volume work compiled by the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}. Based on official histories, ancient texts, and the “Shrine Investigation Records” as well as “Shrine Details Registers” produced in each province during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era, the work, following the order of the Shinmeichō, records the enshrined deities, divine Ranks, shrine status, locations, and other particulars of each {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The editing appears to have been undertaken by {{ill|Kurita Kan|de|Kurita Kan|ja|栗田寛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita Kan|1=Kurita Hiroshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536170}}, {{ill|Konakamura Kiyomune|de|Konakamura Kiyomune|ja|小中村清矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakamura Kiyomune|1=Konakamura Kiyonori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536303}}, {{ill|Kosugi Sanemura|de|Kosugi Sanemura|ja|小杉榲邨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Sanemura|1=Kosugi Sugimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536257}}, {{ill|Inoue Yorimitsu|de|Inoue Yorimitsu|ja|井上頼圀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorimitsu|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535302}}, among others; however, due to the loss of related documents (see below), details remain unclear.<ref>In Kurita’s former library a draft of this work was preserved, and all manuscript copies dating from before the fire were in Kosugi’s own hand, with numerous later additions by Konakamura and Kurita. In addition, participation by then Ministry of Religion members such as {{ill|Tanaka Yoritomo|de|Tanaka Yoritomo|ja|田中頼庸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Yoritomo|1=Yoritsune Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162647}}; {{ill|Ochiai Naotoshi|de|Ochiai Naotoshi|ja|落合直澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochiai Naotoshi|1=Ochiai Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98734451}}; {{ill|Kubo Tokiji|de|Kubo Tokiji|ja|久保季茲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubo Tokiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; {{ill|Monoshū Takami|de|Monoshū Takami|ja|物集高見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshū Takami|1=Mozume Takami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537728}}; {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|de|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}}; {{ill|Nakamura Akika|de|Nakamura Akika|ja|中村秋香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Akika|1=Nakamura Akika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111081324}}; {{ill|Saruwatari Yōsei|de|Saruwatari Yōsei|ja|猿渡容盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruwatari Yōsei|1=Sawatari Hiromori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38279444}}; and {{ill|Hori Hidenari|de|Hori Hidenari|ja|堀秀成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Hidenari|1=Hidenari Hori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120309}} has been surmised.</ref> After its provisional completion in the 9th year of Meiji ({{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}}), the handwritten manuscript was stored in the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} {{ill|Bureau of Shrines|en|Bureau of Shrines|WD=Q11590701|lt=Shrine Bureau|1=Bureau of Shrines}}, but in the 14th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925|1=1925}}) it was published as a western-style single-volume edition by {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
==Compilation History== ===Preliminary History=== In the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era ({{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}), as part of the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, a directive was issued to all shrines nationwide requiring them to submit their genealogies to the local lords in charge. Subsequently, on June 10 of the second year, orders were given to the domains and prefectures to record the histories and shrine legends of the greatly revered major shrines; however, due to slow progress, on February 29 of the third year of Meiji a provisional measure was taken ordering that the portions relating to state‐funded shrines in each domain be submitted to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} by September of that year. These records were then compiled into the “Report on Revered Shrines.”
Furthermore, on October 28 of that leap year, due to the need to formulate shrine regulations, a more detailed survey of the shrines within each jurisdiction was ordered to the domains and prefectures (which eventually took shape as the “Records of Large and Small Shrines”), and based on that survey, in May of the fourth year the Ranks of shrines below the state‐funded level were defined (for details, see ‘’{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}’’).
===Compilation=== [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 175.jpg|thumb|200px|Chapter “Tōmi Country” from the western-style single-volume edition of “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 276.</ref>]] Because the shrine regulations of Meiji 4 established a hierarchical Ranking based on contemporary conditions, some shrines – such as certain {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} – were not granted any Rank (i.e. unranked shrines). Fearing their eventual abolition, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} (the successor to the Jingikan) initiated the compilation of this work.
On April 10 of the 7th year of Meiji, a petition was submitted to the senior officials of the {{ill|Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|de|Daijō-kan (Meiji-Ära)|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|lt=Daijō-kan|lt_ja=太政官|lt_de=Daijō-kan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} requesting the compilation of a work focusing primarily on the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}. The proposal stated that the shrines to be included would be ceremonially enshrined during the two seasonal festivals (spring and autumn) in the three halls of the Imperial Palace – rendered as {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Shrine|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} (at that time, they were enshrined at {{ill|Kensho|de|Kensho|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensho|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}) – and that their shrine grounds would be preserved in accordance with the standards applied to state‐funded shrines. On April 17, a draft imperial edict from the Daijō-kan, ordering each prefecture to conduct a survey of the shrines within their jurisdiction, was submitted. The Daijō-kan, fearing an undue administrative burden on the prefectures, consulted the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} on May 5. As expected, objections were raised on May 14 on the grounds of complicating local administration. Consequently, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} was instructed that while the compilation of this work was permitted, decisions regarding the ceremonial enshrinement at the shrine halls and the preservation of the shrine grounds should await its completion, and that the survey orders to the prefectures should be issued not by the Daijō-kan but by the Ministry of Religion (directive dated May 22).
In June, the Ministry of Religion issued orders to the prefectures to conduct a comprehensive survey of both the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, and to submit their reports by the end of September of that year. Incidentally, the survey covered the following 11 items: # Location of the main seat # Shrine name # Enshrined deity # Genealogy (including the shrine family line – {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}) # Date of enshrinement – {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} # Annual festival date # Building area of the shrine hall # Area of the shrine precincts (including the former precincts) # Former shrine domain – {{ill|Shrine Domain|de|Schreinbereich|ja|社領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=Jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} # Number of registered parishioner households # Distance from the governing authority
Within the Ministry, a compilation committee was established—centered on the Department of Shrines and Temples and the Department of Historical Research—but as the survey demanded even more detailed information than the shrine surveys conducted in Meiji years 2–4, reports were slow to be received even past the deadline, and despite repeated reminders, some prefectures repeatedly requested extensions. Although progress remained slow by August of Meiji 9, in January of the following year the Ministry of Religion was abolished, and despite some omissions, this work was provisionally completed in December of that year.<ref>It is said that Mie Prefecture ultimately did not complete its survey.</ref>
==Publication History== After its completion in Meiji 9, because the manuscript stored in the Ministry of Home Affairs was inconvenient to consult, plans were set in motion to publish it publicly in Taishō 11. While {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was preparing for publication, in September of Taishō 12 the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|de|Großes Kantō-Erdbeben|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} struck during printing. In the ensuing disaster, only four volumes of the original manuscript (the Ministry of Home Affairs version) survived, while all other related documents – including the aforementioned “Report on Revered Shrines” and “Records of Large and Small Shrines” – were completely destroyed.<ref>The only volumes that survived the disaster were Vol. 21 (Echigo/Sado), Vol. 22 (Tango/Tanba), Vol. 23 (Tajima/Inaba/Hakki), and Vol. 24 (Upper Izumo).</ref> Fortunately, the head of Isobe Kōyōdō evacuated with the 580-page paper type that had been approved for printing, and the manuscripts in process were recovered intact from the {{ill|Baggage|en|Baggage|WD=Q865054|lt=Baggage|1=Baggage}}. Based on these, under the supervision of the Ministry of Home Affairs’ temporary appointee {{ill|Ota Akira|de|Ota Akira|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Akira|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, the text was proofread. Although collating it with the original was impossible, the “Shrine Details Register” was used as a reference to append the contemporary (Taishō-era) location and Rank of the shrines, and the work was published. According to the preface, although the original manuscript had included entries for the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, these do not appear in the current edition.

{{Multiple Issues|Source Documentation=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 08:03 (UTC)|Unreferenced=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 08:03 (UTC)}} [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho honbun cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 37.jpg|thumb|300px|‘Tokusen Shinmyōchō’ – Text of the western‐style single-volume edition<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 1.</ref>]]
{{nihongo|'''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'''|特選神名牒}} is a commentary on {{ill|Engi Shikishinmeichō|de|Engi Shikishinmeichō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shikishinmeichō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. It is a 32-volume work compiled by the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}. Based on official histories, ancient texts, and the “Shrine Investigation Records” as well as “Shrine Details Registers” produced in each province during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era, the work, following the order of the Shinmeichō, records the enshrined deities, divine Ranks, shrine status, locations, and other particulars of each {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The editing appears to have been undertaken by {{ill|Kurita Kan|de|Kurita Kan|ja|栗田寛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita Kan|1=Kurita Hiroshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536170}}, {{ill|Konakamura Kiyomune|de|Konakamura Kiyomune|ja|小中村清矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakamura Kiyomune|1=Konakamura Kiyonori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536303}}, {{ill|Kosugi Sanemura|de|Kosugi Sanemura|ja|小杉榲邨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Sanemura|1=Kosugi Sugimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536257}}, {{ill|Inoue Yorimitsu|de|Inoue Yorimitsu|ja|井上頼圀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorimitsu|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535302}}, among others; however, due to the loss of related documents (see below), details remain unclear.<ref>In Kurita’s former library a draft of this work was preserved, and all manuscript copies dating from before the fire were in Kosugi’s own hand, with numerous later additions by Konakamura and Kurita. In addition, participation by then Ministry of Religion members such as {{ill|Tanaka Yoritomo|de|Tanaka Yoritomo|ja|田中頼庸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Yoritomo|1=Yoritsune Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162647}}; {{ill|Ochiai Naotoshi|de|Ochiai Naotoshi|ja|落合直澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochiai Naotoshi|1=Ochiai Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98734451}}; {{ill|Kubo Tokiji|de|Kubo Tokiji|ja|久保季茲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubo Tokiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; {{ill|Monoshū Takami|de|Monoshū Takami|ja|物集高見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshū Takami|1=Mozume Takami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537728}}; {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|de|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}}; {{ill|Nakamura Akika|de|Nakamura Akika|ja|中村秋香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Akika|1=Nakamura Akika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111081324}}; {{ill|Saruwatari Yōsei|de|Saruwatari Yōsei|ja|猿渡容盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruwatari Yōsei|1=Sawatari Hiromori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38279444}}; and {{ill|Hori Hidenari|de|Hori Hidenari|ja|堀秀成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Hidenari|1=Hidenari Hori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120309}} has been surmised.</ref> After its provisional completion in the 9th year of Meiji ({{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}}), the handwritten manuscript was stored in the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} {{ill|Bureau of Shrines|en|Bureau of Shrines|WD=Q11590701|lt=Shrine Bureau|1=Bureau of Shrines}}, but in the 14th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925|1=1925}}) it was published as a western-style single-volume edition by {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
==Compilation History== ===Preliminary History=== In the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era ({{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}), as part of the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, a directive was issued to all shrines nationwide requiring them to submit their genealogies to the local lords in charge. Subsequently, on June 10 of the second year, orders were given to the domains and prefectures to record the histories and shrine legends of the greatly revered major shrines; however, due to slow progress, on February 29 of the third year of Meiji a provisional measure was taken ordering that the portions relating to state‐funded shrines in each domain be submitted to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} by September of that year. These records were then compiled into the “Report on Revered Shrines.”
Furthermore, on October 28 of that leap year, due to the need to formulate shrine regulations, a more detailed survey of the shrines within each jurisdiction was ordered to the domains and prefectures (which eventually took shape as the “Records of Large and Small Shrines”), and based on that survey, in May of the fourth year the Ranks of shrines below the state‐funded level were defined (for details, see ‘’{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}’’).
===Compilation=== [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 175.jpg|thumb|200px|Chapter “Tōmi Country” from the western-style single-volume edition of “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 276.</ref>]] Because the shrine regulations of Meiji 4 established a hierarchical Ranking based on contemporary conditions, some shrines – such as certain {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} – were not granted any Rank (i.e. unranked shrines). Fearing their eventual abolition, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} (the successor to the Jingikan) initiated the compilation of this work.
On April 10 of the 7th year of Meiji, a petition was submitted to the senior officials of the {{ill|Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|de|Daijō-kan (Meiji-Ära)|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|lt=Daijō-kan|lt_ja=太政官|lt_de=Daijō-kan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} requesting the compilation of a work focusing primarily on the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}. The proposal stated that the shrines to be included would be ceremonially enshrined during the two seasonal festivals (spring and autumn) in the three halls of the Imperial Palace – rendered as {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Shrine|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} (at that time, they were enshrined at {{ill|Kensho|de|Kensho|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensho|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}) – and that their shrine grounds would be preserved in accordance with the standards applied to state‐funded shrines. On April 17, a draft imperial edict from the Daijō-kan, ordering each prefecture to conduct a survey of the shrines within their jurisdiction, was submitted. The Daijō-kan, fearing an undue administrative burden on the prefectures, consulted the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} on May 5. As expected, objections were raised on May 14 on the grounds of complicating local administration. Consequently, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} was instructed that while the compilation of this work was permitted, decisions regarding the ceremonial enshrinement at the shrine halls and the preservation of the shrine grounds should await its completion, and that the survey orders to the prefectures should be issued not by the Daijō-kan but by the Ministry of Religion (directive dated May 22).
In June, the Ministry of Religion issued orders to the prefectures to conduct a comprehensive survey of both the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, and to submit their reports by the end of September of that year. Incidentally, the survey covered the following 11 items: # Location of the main seat # Shrine name # Enshrined deity # Genealogy (including the shrine family line – {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}) # Date of enshrinement – {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} # Annual festival date # Building area of the shrine hall # Area of the shrine precincts (including the former precincts) # Former shrine domain – {{ill|Shrine Domain|de|Schreinbereich|ja|社領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=Jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} # Number of registered parishioner households # Distance from the governing authority
Within the Ministry, a compilation committee was established—centered on the Department of Shrines and Temples and the Department of Historical Research—but as the survey demanded even more detailed information than the shrine surveys conducted in Meiji years 2–4, reports were slow to be received even past the deadline, and despite repeated reminders, some prefectures repeatedly requested extensions. Although progress remained slow by August of Meiji 9, in January of the following year the Ministry of Religion was abolished, and despite some omissions, this work was provisionally completed in December of that year.<ref>It is said that Mie Prefecture ultimately did not complete its survey.</ref>
==Publication History== After its completion in Meiji 9, because the manuscript stored in the Ministry of Home Affairs was inconvenient to consult, plans were set in motion to publish it publicly in Taishō 11. While {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was preparing for publication, in September of Taishō 12 the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|de|Großes Kantō-Erdbeben|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} struck during printing. In the ensuing disaster, only four volumes of the original manuscript (the Ministry of Home Affairs version) survived, while all other related documents – including the aforementioned “Report on Revered Shrines” and “Records of Large and Small Shrines” – were completely destroyed.<ref>The only volumes that survived the disaster were Vol. 21 (Echigo/Sado), Vol. 22 (Tango/Tanba), Vol. 23 (Tajima/Inaba/Hakki), and Vol. 24 (Upper Izumo).</ref> Fortunately, the head of Isobe Kōyōdō evacuated with the 580-page paper type that had been approved for printing, and the manuscripts in process were recovered intact from the {{ill|Baggage|en|Baggage|WD=Q865054|lt=Baggage|1=Baggage}}. Based on these, under the supervision of the Ministry of Home Affairs’ temporary appointee {{ill|Ota Akira|de|Ota Akira|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Akira|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, the text was proofread. Although collating it with the original was impossible, the “Shrine Details Register” was used as a reference to append the contemporary (Taishō-era) location and Rank of the shrines, and the work was published. According to the preface, although the original manuscript had included entries for the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, these do not appear in the current edition.

In addition, participation by then Ministry of Religion members such as {{ill|Tanaka Yoritomo|de|Tanaka Yoritomo|ja|田中頼庸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Yoritomo|1=Yoritsune Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162647}}; {{ill|Nakamura Akika|de|Nakamura Akika|ja|中村秋香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Akika|1=Nakamura Akika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111081324}}; {{ill|Saruwatari Yōsei|de|Saruwatari Yōsei|ja|猿渡容盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruwatari Yōsei|1=Sawatari Hiromori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38279444}}; and {{ill|Hori Hidenari|de|Hori Hidenari|ja|堀秀成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Hidenari|1=Hidenari Hori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120309}} has been surmised.</ref> After its provisional completion in the 9th year of Meiji ({{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}}), the handwritten manuscript was stored in the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} {{ill|Bureau of Shrines|en|Bureau of Shrines|WD=Q11590701|lt=Shrine Bureau|1=Bureau of Shrines}}, but in the 14th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925|1=1925}}) it was published as a western-style single-volume edition by {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
==Compilation History== ===Preliminary History=== In the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era ({{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}), as part of the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, a directive was issued to all shrines nationwide requiring them to submit their genealogies to the local lords in charge. Subsequently, on June 10 of the second year, orders were given to the domains and prefectures to record the histories and shrine legends of the greatly revered major shrines; however, due to slow progress, on February 29 of the third year of Meiji a provisional measure was taken ordering that the portions relating to state‐funded shrines in each domain be submitted to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} by September of that year. These records were then compiled into the “Report on Revered Shrines.”
Furthermore, on October 28 of that leap year, due to the need to formulate shrine regulations, a more detailed survey of the shrines within each jurisdiction was ordered to the domains and prefectures (which eventually took shape as the “Records of Large and Small Shrines”), and based on that survey, in May of the fourth year the Ranks of shrines below the state‐funded level were defined (for details, see ‘’{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}’’).
===Compilation=== [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 175.jpg|thumb|200px|Chapter “Tōmi Country” from the western-style single-volume edition of “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 276.</ref>]] Because the shrine regulations of Meiji 4 established a hierarchical Ranking based on contemporary conditions, some shrines – such as certain {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} – were not granted any Rank (i.e. unranked shrines). Fearing their eventual abolition, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} (the successor to the Jingikan) initiated the compilation of this work.
On April 10 of the 7th year of Meiji, a petition was submitted to the senior officials of the {{ill|Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|de|Daijō-kan (Meiji-Ära)|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|lt=Daijō-kan|lt_ja=太政官|lt_de=Daijō-kan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} requesting the compilation of a work focusing primarily on the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}. The proposal stated that the shrines to be included would be ceremonially enshrined during the two seasonal festivals (spring and autumn) in the three halls of the Imperial Palace – rendered as {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Shrine|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} (at that time, they were enshrined at {{ill|Kensho|de|Kensho|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensho|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}) – and that their shrine grounds would be preserved in accordance with the standards applied to state‐funded shrines. On April 17, a draft imperial edict from the Daijō-kan, ordering each prefecture to conduct a survey of the shrines within their jurisdiction, was submitted. The Daijō-kan, fearing an undue administrative burden on the prefectures, consulted the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} on May 5. As expected, objections were raised on May 14 on the grounds of complicating local administration. Consequently, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} was instructed that while the compilation of this work was permitted, decisions regarding the ceremonial enshrinement at the shrine halls and the preservation of the shrine grounds should await its completion, and that the survey orders to the prefectures should be issued not by the Daijō-kan but by the Ministry of Religion (directive dated May 22).
In June, the Ministry of Religion issued orders to the prefectures to conduct a comprehensive survey of both the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, and to submit their reports by the end of September of that year. Incidentally, the survey covered the following 11 items: # Location of the main seat # Shrine name # Enshrined deity # Genealogy (including the shrine family line – {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}) # Date of enshrinement – {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} # Annual festival date # Building area of the shrine hall # Area of the shrine precincts (including the former precincts) # Former shrine domain – {{ill|Shrine Domain|de|Schreinbereich|ja|社領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=Jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} # Number of registered parishioner households # Distance from the governing authority
Within the Ministry, a compilation committee was established—centered on the Department of Shrines and Temples and the Department of Historical Research—but as the survey demanded even more detailed information than the shrine surveys conducted in Meiji years 2–4, reports were slow to be received even past the deadline, and despite repeated reminders, some prefectures repeatedly requested extensions. Although progress remained slow by August of Meiji 9, in January of the following year the Ministry of Religion was abolished, and despite some omissions, this work was provisionally completed in December of that year.<ref>It is said that Mie Prefecture ultimately did not complete its survey.</ref>
==Publication History== After its completion in Meiji 9, because the manuscript stored in the Ministry of Home Affairs was inconvenient to consult, plans were set in motion to publish it publicly in Taishō 11. While {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was preparing for publication, in September of Taishō 12 the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|de|Großes Kantō-Erdbeben|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} struck during printing. In the ensuing disaster, only four volumes of the original manuscript (the Ministry of Home Affairs version) survived, while all other related documents – including the aforementioned “Report on Revered Shrines” and “Records of Large and Small Shrines” – were completely destroyed.<ref>The only volumes that survived the disaster were Vol. 21 (Echigo/Sado), Vol. 22 (Tango/Tanba), Vol. 23 (Tajima/Inaba/Hakki), and Vol. 24 (Upper Izumo).</ref> Fortunately, the head of Isobe Kōyōdō evacuated with the 580-page paper type that had been approved for printing, and the manuscripts in process were recovered intact from the {{ill|Baggage|en|Baggage|WD=Q865054|lt=Baggage|1=Baggage}}. Based on these, under the supervision of the Ministry of Home Affairs’ temporary appointee {{ill|Ota Akira|de|Ota Akira|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Akira|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, the text was proofread. Although collating it with the original was impossible, the “Shrine Details Register” was used as a reference to append the contemporary (Taishō-era) location and Rank of the shrines, and the work was published. According to the preface, although the original manuscript had included entries for the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, these do not appear in the current edition.

{{Multiple Issues|Source Documentation=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 08:03 (UTC)|Unreferenced=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 08:03 (UTC)}} [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho honbun cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 37.jpg|thumb|300px|‘Tokusen Shinmyōchō’ – Text of the western‐style single-volume edition<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 1.</ref>]]
{{nihongo|'''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'''|特選神名牒}} is a commentary on {{ill|Engi Shikishinmeichō|de|Engi Shikishinmeichō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shikishinmeichō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. It is a 32-volume work compiled by the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}. Based on official histories, ancient texts, and the “Shrine Investigation Records” as well as “Shrine Details Registers” produced in each province during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era, the work, following the order of the Shinmeichō, records the enshrined deities, divine Ranks, shrine status, locations, and other particulars of each {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The editing appears to have been undertaken by {{ill|Kurita Kan|de|Kurita Kan|ja|栗田寛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita Kan|1=Kurita Hiroshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536170}}, {{ill|Konakamura Kiyomune|de|Konakamura Kiyomune|ja|小中村清矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakamura Kiyomune|1=Konakamura Kiyonori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536303}}, {{ill|Kosugi Sanemura|de|Kosugi Sanemura|ja|小杉榲邨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Sanemura|1=Kosugi Sugimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536257}}, {{ill|Inoue Yorimitsu|de|Inoue Yorimitsu|ja|井上頼圀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorimitsu|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535302}}, among others; however, due to the loss of related documents (see below), details remain unclear.<ref>In Kurita’s former library a draft of this work was preserved, and all manuscript copies dating from before the fire were in Kosugi’s own hand, with numerous later additions by Konakamura and Kurita. In addition, participation by then Ministry of Religion members such as {{ill|Tanaka Yoritomo|de|Tanaka Yoritomo|ja|田中頼庸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Yoritomo|1=Yoritsune Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162647}}; {{ill|Ochiai Naotoshi|de|Ochiai Naotoshi|ja|落合直澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochiai Naotoshi|1=Ochiai Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98734451}}; {{ill|Kubo Tokiji|de|Kubo Tokiji|ja|久保季茲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubo Tokiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; {{ill|Monoshū Takami|de|Monoshū Takami|ja|物集高見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshū Takami|1=Mozume Takami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537728}}; {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|de|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}}; {{ill|Nakamura Akika|de|Nakamura Akika|ja|中村秋香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Akika|1=Nakamura Akika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111081324}}; {{ill|Saruwatari Yōsei|de|Saruwatari Yōsei|ja|猿渡容盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruwatari Yōsei|1=Sawatari Hiromori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38279444}}; and {{ill|Hori Hidenari|de|Hori Hidenari|ja|堀秀成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Hidenari|1=Hidenari Hori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120309}} has been surmised.</ref> After its provisional completion in the 9th year of Meiji ({{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}}), the handwritten manuscript was stored in the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} {{ill|Bureau of Shrines|en|Bureau of Shrines|WD=Q11590701|lt=Shrine Bureau|1=Bureau of Shrines}}, but in the 14th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925|1=1925}}) it was published as a western-style single-volume edition by {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
==Compilation History== ===Preliminary History=== In the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era ({{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}), as part of the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, a directive was issued to all shrines nationwide requiring them to submit their genealogies to the local lords in charge. Subsequently, on June 10 of the second year, orders were given to the domains and prefectures to record the histories and shrine legends of the greatly revered major shrines; however, due to slow progress, on February 29 of the third year of Meiji a provisional measure was taken ordering that the portions relating to state‐funded shrines in each domain be submitted to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} by September of that year. These records were then compiled into the “Report on Revered Shrines.”
Furthermore, on October 28 of that leap year, due to the need to formulate shrine regulations, a more detailed survey of the shrines within each jurisdiction was ordered to the domains and prefectures (which eventually took shape as the “Records of Large and Small Shrines”), and based on that survey, in May of the fourth year the Ranks of shrines below the state‐funded level were defined (for details, see ‘’{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}’’).

As expected, objections were raised on May 14 on the grounds of complicating local administration. Consequently, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} was instructed that while the compilation of this work was permitted, decisions regarding the ceremonial enshrinement at the shrine halls and the preservation of the shrine grounds should await its completion, and that the survey orders to the prefectures should be issued not by the Daijō-kan but by the Ministry of Religion (directive dated May 22).
In June, the Ministry of Religion issued orders to the prefectures to conduct a comprehensive survey of both the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, and to submit their reports by the end of September of that year. Incidentally, the survey covered the following 11 items: # Location of the main seat # Shrine name # Enshrined deity # Genealogy (including the shrine family line – {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}) # Date of enshrinement – {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} # Annual festival date # Building area of the shrine hall # Area of the shrine precincts (including the former precincts) # Former shrine domain – {{ill|Shrine Domain|de|Schreinbereich|ja|社領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=Jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} # Number of registered parishioner households # Distance from the governing authority
Within the Ministry, a compilation committee was established—centered on the Department of Shrines and Temples and the Department of Historical Research—but as the survey demanded even more detailed information than the shrine surveys conducted in Meiji years 2–4, reports were slow to be received even past the deadline, and despite repeated reminders, some prefectures repeatedly requested extensions. Although progress remained slow by August of Meiji 9, in January of the following year the Ministry of Religion was abolished, and despite some omissions, this work was provisionally completed in December of that year.<ref>It is said that Mie Prefecture ultimately did not complete its survey.</ref>
==Publication History== After its completion in Meiji 9, because the manuscript stored in the Ministry of Home Affairs was inconvenient to consult, plans were set in motion to publish it publicly in Taishō 11. While {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was preparing for publication, in September of Taishō 12 the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|de|Großes Kantō-Erdbeben|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} struck during printing. In the ensuing disaster, only four volumes of the original manuscript (the Ministry of Home Affairs version) survived, while all other related documents – including the aforementioned “Report on Revered Shrines” and “Records of Large and Small Shrines” – were completely destroyed.<ref>The only volumes that survived the disaster were Vol. 21 (Echigo/Sado), Vol. 22 (Tango/Tanba), Vol. 23 (Tajima/Inaba/Hakki), and Vol. 24 (Upper Izumo).</ref> Fortunately, the head of Isobe Kōyōdō evacuated with the 580-page paper type that had been approved for printing, and the manuscripts in process were recovered intact from the {{ill|Baggage|en|Baggage|WD=Q865054|lt=Baggage|1=Baggage}}. Based on these, under the supervision of the Ministry of Home Affairs’ temporary appointee {{ill|Ota Akira|de|Ota Akira|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Akira|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, the text was proofread. Although collating it with the original was impossible, the “Shrine Details Register” was used as a reference to append the contemporary (Taishō-era) location and Rank of the shrines, and the work was published. According to the preface, although the original manuscript had included entries for the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, these do not appear in the current edition.
==Footnotes==
{{Reflist|2}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tokusen Jimmyōchō]][[ja:特選神名牒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tokusen Jimmyōchō]][[ja:特選神名牒]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:特選神名牒]]
{{Multiple Issues|Source Documentation=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 08:03 (UTC)|Unreferenced=August 28, 2017 (Mon) 08:03 (UTC)}} [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho honbun cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 37.jpg|thumb|300px|‘Tokusen Shinmyōchō’ – Text of the western‐style single-volume edition<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 1.</ref>]]
{{nihongo|'''Tokusen Jinmyōchō'''|特選神名牒}} is a commentary on {{ill|Engi Shikishinmeichō|de|Engi Shikishinmeichō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shikishinmeichō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. It is a 32-volume work compiled by the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}}. Based on official histories, ancient texts, and the “Shrine Investigation Records” as well as “Shrine Details Registers” produced in each province during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era, the work, following the order of the Shinmeichō, records the enshrined deities, divine Ranks, shrine status, locations, and other particulars of each {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. The editing appears to have been undertaken by {{ill|Kurita Kan|de|Kurita Kan|ja|栗田寛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurita Kan|1=Kurita Hiroshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536170}}, {{ill|Konakamura Kiyomune|de|Konakamura Kiyomune|ja|小中村清矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konakamura Kiyomune|1=Konakamura Kiyonori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536303}}, {{ill|Kosugi Sanemura|de|Kosugi Sanemura|ja|小杉榲邨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosugi Sanemura|1=Kosugi Sugimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536257}}, {{ill|Inoue Yorimitsu|de|Inoue Yorimitsu|ja|井上頼圀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Yorimitsu|1=Inoue Yorikuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6535302}}, among others; however, due to the loss of related documents (see below), details remain unclear.<ref>In Kurita’s former library a draft of this work was preserved, and all manuscript copies dating from before the fire were in Kosugi’s own hand, with numerous later additions by Konakamura and Kurita. In addition, participation by then Ministry of Religion members such as {{ill|Tanaka Yoritomo|de|Tanaka Yoritomo|ja|田中頼庸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanaka Yoritomo|1=Yoritsune Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162647}}; {{ill|Ochiai Naotoshi|de|Ochiai Naotoshi|ja|落合直澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochiai Naotoshi|1=Ochiai Naozumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q98734451}}; {{ill|Kubo Tokiji|de|Kubo Tokiji|ja|久保季茲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubo Tokiji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; {{ill|Monoshū Takami|de|Monoshū Takami|ja|物集高見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshū Takami|1=Mozume Takami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537728}}; {{ill|Tokoyo Nagatane|de|Tokoyo Nagatane|ja|常世長胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Nagatane|1=Tokoyo Nagatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6536975}}; {{ill|Nakamura Akika|de|Nakamura Akika|ja|中村秋香|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Akika|1=Nakamura Akika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111081324}}; {{ill|Saruwatari Yōsei|de|Saruwatari Yōsei|ja|猿渡容盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruwatari Yōsei|1=Sawatari Hiromori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38279444}}; and {{ill|Hori Hidenari|de|Hori Hidenari|ja|堀秀成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hori Hidenari|1=Hidenari Hori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120309}} has been surmised.</ref> After its provisional completion in the 9th year of Meiji ({{ill|1876|en|1876|WD=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}}), the handwritten manuscript was stored in the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} {{ill|Bureau of Shrines|en|Bureau of Shrines|WD=Q11590701|lt=Shrine Bureau|1=Bureau of Shrines}}, but in the 14th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} ({{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925|1=1925}}) it was published as a western-style single-volume edition by {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
==Compilation History== ===Preliminary History=== In the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era ({{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}), as part of the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, a directive was issued to all shrines nationwide requiring them to submit their genealogies to the local lords in charge. Subsequently, on June 10 of the second year, orders were given to the domains and prefectures to record the histories and shrine legends of the greatly revered major shrines; however, due to slow progress, on February 29 of the third year of Meiji a provisional measure was taken ordering that the portions relating to state‐funded shrines in each domain be submitted to the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|WD=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} by September of that year. These records were then compiled into the “Report on Revered Shrines.”
Furthermore, on October 28 of that leap year, due to the need to formulate shrine regulations, a more detailed survey of the shrines within each jurisdiction was ordered to the domains and prefectures (which eventually took shape as the “Records of Large and Small Shrines”), and based on that survey, in May of the fourth year the Ranks of shrines below the state‐funded level were defined (for details, see ‘’{{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}’’).
===Compilation=== [[File:Tokusen Jimmyocho cropped Tokusen Jimmyocho Frame 175.jpg|thumb|200px|Chapter “Tōmi Country” from the western-style single-volume edition of “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”<ref>{{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} compiled “Tokusen Shinmyōchō”, {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, 1925, p. 276.</ref>]] Because the shrine regulations of Meiji 4 established a hierarchical Ranking based on contemporary conditions, some shrines – such as certain {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} – were not granted any Rank (i.e. unranked shrines). Fearing their eventual abolition, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} (the successor to the Jingikan) initiated the compilation of this work.
On April 10 of the 7th year of Meiji, a petition was submitted to the senior officials of the {{ill|Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|de|Daijō-kan (Meiji-Ära)|ja|太政官 (明治時代)|lt=Daijō-kan|lt_ja=太政官|lt_de=Daijō-kan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan (Meiji era)|1=Dajōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125768223}} requesting the compilation of a work focusing primarily on the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}. The proposal stated that the shrines to be included would be ceremonially enshrined during the two seasonal festivals (spring and autumn) in the three halls of the Imperial Palace – rendered as {{ill|Three Palace Sanctuaries|en|Three Palace Sanctuaries|WD=Q7797685|lt=Shrine|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries}} (at that time, they were enshrined at {{ill|Kensho|de|Kensho|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kensho|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}) – and that their shrine grounds would be preserved in accordance with the standards applied to state‐funded shrines. On April 17, a draft imperial edict from the Daijō-kan, ordering each prefecture to conduct a survey of the shrines within their jurisdiction, was submitted. The Daijō-kan, fearing an undue administrative burden on the prefectures, consulted the {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|WD=Q1142265|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs|1=Home Ministry}} on May 5. As expected, objections were raised on May 14 on the grounds of complicating local administration. Consequently, the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} was instructed that while the compilation of this work was permitted, decisions regarding the ceremonial enshrinement at the shrine halls and the preservation of the shrine grounds should await its completion, and that the survey orders to the prefectures should be issued not by the Daijō-kan but by the Ministry of Religion (directive dated May 22).
In June, the Ministry of Religion issued orders to the prefectures to conduct a comprehensive survey of both the {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, and to submit their reports by the end of September of that year. Incidentally, the survey covered the following 11 items: # Location of the main seat # Shrine name # Enshrined deity # Genealogy (including the shrine family line – {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}}) # Date of enshrinement – {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} # Annual festival date # Building area of the shrine hall # Area of the shrine precincts (including the former precincts) # Former shrine domain – {{ill|Shrine Domain|de|Schreinbereich|ja|社領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=Jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} # Number of registered parishioner households # Distance from the governing authority
Within the Ministry, a compilation committee was established—centered on the Department of Shrines and Temples and the Department of Historical Research—but as the survey demanded even more detailed information than the shrine surveys conducted in Meiji years 2–4, reports were slow to be received even past the deadline, and despite repeated reminders, some prefectures repeatedly requested extensions. Although progress remained slow by August of Meiji 9, in January of the following year the Ministry of Religion was abolished, and despite some omissions, this work was provisionally completed in December of that year.<ref>It is said that Mie Prefecture ultimately did not complete its survey.</ref>
==Publication History== After its completion in Meiji 9, because the manuscript stored in the Ministry of Home Affairs was inconvenient to consult, plans were set in motion to publish it publicly in Taishō 11. While {{ill|Isobe Kōyōdō|de|Isobe Kōyōdō|ja|磯部甲陽堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe Kōyōdō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was preparing for publication, in September of Taishō 12 the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|de|Großes Kantō-Erdbeben|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}} struck during printing. In the ensuing disaster, only four volumes of the original manuscript (the Ministry of Home Affairs version) survived, while all other related documents – including the aforementioned “Report on Revered Shrines” and “Records of Large and Small Shrines” – were completely destroyed.<ref>The only volumes that survived the disaster were Vol. 21 (Echigo/Sado), Vol. 22 (Tango/Tanba), Vol. 23 (Tajima/Inaba/Hakki), and Vol. 24 (Upper Izumo).</ref> Fortunately, the head of Isobe Kōyōdō evacuated with the 580-page paper type that had been approved for printing, and the manuscripts in process were recovered intact from the {{ill|Baggage|en|Baggage|WD=Q865054|lt=Baggage|1=Baggage}}. Based on these, under the supervision of the Ministry of Home Affairs’ temporary appointee {{ill|Ota Akira|de|Ota Akira|ja|太田亮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ota Akira|1=Akira Ōta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13099367}}, the text was proofread. Although collating it with the original was impossible, the “Shrine Details Register” was used as a reference to append the contemporary (Taishō-era) location and Rank of the shrines, and the work was published. According to the preface, although the original manuscript had included entries for the {{ill|Kokushi Mitaisha|de|Kokushi Mitaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Mitaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}}, these do not appear in the current edition.
==Footnotes==
